chapter.title: A brief life of saint john CHRYSOSTOM

paragraphs.text: PATRIARCH OF CONSTANTINOPLE it to one of the archdeacons. He told him to go and read it over the brother. It was a prayer absolving the dead man (rom exclusion.

It was said of Saint John of Constantinople, justly known as The archdeacon went as he was commanded and read the letter Chrysostom <=golden mouth> for the purity and the brilliance of containing the prayer over the brother. That same night the his teaching and the splendour of his eloquence, that from the day higoumen saw the dead brother. The higoumen said to him: ‘Are he received the salutary sacrament of baptism, he neither swore nor you not dead, brother? and he said: ‘Yes, indeed’. Then the abba required anybody else to swear, nor fied, nor spoke, nor listened to asked him again: ‘Where were you until today?” The brother said to witty words. him: ‘Teuly, sir, I was in prison and I was not set free until yesterday’.
